zh.lifeSummary: 當Andlaw Fitz Hemming of France於1010年出生在法國皮卡迪索姆時，他的父親Heming Håkonsson42歲，母親Brita Johansdatter22歲。他和Guri of Alba至少有2個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Andlaw Fitz Hemming of France于1010年出生在法国皮卡迪索姆时，他的父亲Heming Håkonsson42岁，母亲Brita Johansdatter22岁。他和Guri of Alba至少有2个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Hemming
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Swedish: from the Old Norse personal name Hemingr, Hem(m)ing, of uncertain origin but apparently related to Old Norse hemingr ‘skin of the shanks (hind legs of an animal)’.
German: patronymic from   Hemme   1.

variants.name: Hemmings
meanings: English:
variant of Hemming , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
in some cases a late variant of Emmins, itself related to an alternative form of the Middle English female personal name Emeny (see Edmond 2).

variants.name: Hemmingson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Lars.
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Hemmingsson: patronymic from the personal name Hemming .
Americanized form of Danish and Norwegian   Hemmingsen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Heming
meanings: English and German: variant of   Hemming  .

variants.name: Hemmingsen
meanings: Danish and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Hemming  .

variants.name: Hemme
meanings: North German and Dutch (northeastern Netherlands):
from a short form of the personal name Hemmert derived from an ancient Germanic compound name formed with heim ‘home’ + berht ‘bright, illustrious’.
habitational name from a place called Hemme in Holstein.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: France
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from France (see 2 below).

French: habitational name given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris); or, in southern France, an ethnic name for someone from what is now the northern half of the country, where the langue d'oïl (Old French as distinct from Occitan) was spoken. Compare   Defrance   and   Lafrance  .
Czech (Franče): from a pet form of František, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Defrance
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Henri, Jean-Paul.

French (mainly northern): habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris). Compare France .

en.lifeSummary: When Andlaw Fitz Hemming of France was born in 1010, in Somme, Picardie, France, his father, Heming Håkonsson, was 42 and his mother, Brita Johansdatter, was 22. He had at least 2 daughters with Guri of Alba.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Andlaw Fitz Hemming of France est né en 1010, à Somme, Picardie, France, son père, Heming Håkonsson, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Brita Johansdatter, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Guri of Alba.

de.lifeSummary: Als Andlaw Fitz Hemming of France 1010 in Somme, Picardie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Heming Håkonsson 42 und seine Mutter Brita Johansdatter 22. Er hatte mit Guri of Alba mindestens 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Andlaw Fitz Hemming of France è nato il nel 1010 nel Somme, Picardie, Francia, il suo padre, Heming Håkonsson, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Brita Johansdatter, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Guri of Alba.

ko.lifeSummary: Andlaw Fitz Hemming of France(이)가 1010년 프랑스 피카디 솜에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Heming Håkonsson(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Brita Johansdatter(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Guri of Alba(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Andlaw Fitz Hemming of France nasceu em 1010, em Somme, Picardie, França, seu pai, Heming Håkonsson, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Brita Johansdatter, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Guri of Alba.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Andlaw Fitz Hemming of France родился в 1010 г. в местности сомм, пикарди, Франция, его отцу по имени Heming Håkonsson было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Brita Johansdatter, 22. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Guri of Alba.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Andlaw Fitz Hemming of France nació en 1010, en Somme, Picardie, Francia, su papá, Heming Håkonsson, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Brita Johansdatter, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Guri of Alba.
